$schema: Each entry: { brief: 'one-line plain-language', more?: 'extended', kind?: 'tool|concept|process|acronym|tech' }. The LearnTerm component renders the brief on hover/tap and 'more' if expanded. Strategy Claude or future sessions can extend this without touching code.

CLAUDE.md.brief: A markdown file at the root of your project that Claude reads at the start of every session. It tells Claude what you're building, your conventions, and what to avoid.
CLAUDE.md.more: Most people skip this. The ones who use it get dramatically better Claude output because they stop re-explaining their project on every chat. Yours is generated as part of the 60-minute setup and updates as your project grows.

Nexus map.brief: A live visual map of every tool, repo, and process in your AI workspace. Auto-syncs from GitHub so new repos appear automatically.
Nexus map.more: It's the bird's-eye-view answer to 'what do I actually have set up, and what's missing?' The dashboard pulls real connection state from your accounts so it stays accurate without you maintaining it.

prompt library.brief: A curated set of prompts tuned to your tone and your project, organized by job-to-be-done. You copy, edit, run.
prompt library.more: Each prompt has a 'why it works' note explaining the reasoning, so you can adapt them without copying blind. The library updates as Claude's capabilities change.

Workspace Pulse.brief: The live dashboard you get after the 60-minute setup. Tracks Sessions, Commits, Prompts, Decisions, and Hours saved so you can see your AI workspace working.
Workspace Pulse.more: Same five streams every customer sees, fed by your own repos and tools. Pattern signals (stuck patterns, drift, plateau risk) tell you when something needs attention.

decisions log.brief: A running file of locked choices with rationale, so you stop re-debating settled questions.
decisions log.more: Every entry is timestamped and short. Read it when you feel a debate coming on; if the answer is already there, move on.

starter repo.brief: A pre-built code repository you fork to your GitHub account, with the Claude integration, env config, and deploy pipeline already wired up.
starter repo.more: Saves the 4-6 hours it normally takes to wire up the same pieces from scratch. You paste your API keys and it's running on Vercel in minutes.

Claude.brief: Anthropic's AI assistant. The thing this onboarding configures for your specific work.

Claude Code.brief: The terminal-based version of Claude that can read and write files in your project. Different from Claude.ai, the chat website.
Claude Code.more: Claude Code understands the structure of your repo and can make multi-file changes in one session. Reads CLAUDE.md at startup so it knows your conventions.

GitHub.brief: Where your code lives. Free for individuals; the starter repo gets pushed here when you fork it.

Vercel.brief: The platform that hosts your live site. Free tier covers most solo projects; pushing to your repo's main branch deploys automatically.

Stripe.brief: Payment processor. Used here just for the $49 checkout link.

1Password.brief: Password manager. The setup pipes API keys through it so they never live in plain text on your machine.

Cursor.brief: An AI-first code editor. Optional but recommended; works alongside Claude Code.

MCP server.brief: Model Context Protocol server. A small program that gives Claude access to one specific external thing (your calendar, your notes, a database).
MCP server.more: Think 'plugin' but standardized. The setup walks you through installing two or three that match your work; you can add more later.

fork.brief: A copy of someone else's GitHub repo, owned by you, that you can change without affecting the original.

repo.brief: Short for repository. A folder of code tracked by git, usually hosted on GitHub.

API key.brief: A secret string that lets your app call a service (Claude, GitHub, Stripe). Treated like a password.
API key.more: The setup walks you through generating each key and storing it where it belongs (1Password + your project's environment variables, never in source code).

env var.brief: Short for environment variable. A configuration value (like an API key) that lives outside your code so it can change per environment.

webhook.brief: A URL another service POSTs to when something happens. Used here for the email subscribe and the newsletter publish flow.

session.brief: One conversation with Claude, from open to close. Sessions per week is one of the five Workspace Pulse streams.

commit.brief: A saved snapshot of your code in git, with a short message describing what changed. Commits per week is one of the Workspace Pulse streams.

stuck pattern.brief: A prompt you used a lot last month and stopped using this month. Surfaces in the Pattern signals panel because it usually means the prompt needs an update.

drift.brief: When sessions start asking Claude clarifying questions instead of moving the work forward, your CLAUDE.md is probably out of date.
drift.more: Drift detected: 12% means 12% of recent sessions had this pattern. The fix is usually a 5-minute CLAUDE.md update.

plateau risk.brief: Weeks since you added a new prompt to your library. Most people who churn from AI tools were on a plateau for 3+ weeks first.

active alerts.brief: Items the dashboard flagged that need attention. The number on the dashboard tells you how many 5-minute fixes are queued up.

tone.brief: How your prompts read. The setup interviews you to capture your voice so the library doesn't sound like generic ChatGPT output.
tone.more: Candid, warm, brisk, sober — pick the closest match or describe your own. The same prompt template comes out differently in different tones.

onboarding.brief: The 60-minute guided setup that turns this product into your working AI workspace.

the five phases.brief: Capture your idea (5 min), set up accounts (15 min), install the starter (10 min), configure Claude (20 min), receive your outputs (10 min).

lifetime access.brief: One $49 payment, no subscription. You keep the repo, the spec, the prompts, and every future update.
lifetime access.more: Updates ship to your repo and library when Claude releases new capabilities. You don't have to track the news; the system does.

command palette.brief: A search bar that appears when you press ⌘K (or Ctrl+K on Windows). Jumps you to any section, prompt, or FAQ on the site.

Learn mode.brief: These dotted-underline tooltips. On by default; toggle off in Settings or via the chip in the utility bar once you don't need them anymore.

AI workspace.brief: The collection of tools, files, and conventions Claude reads at the start of every session. Built once during onboarding, refined continuously after.

the de facto AI lead.brief: The person on your team who became the AI person by default — usually because nobody else volunteered. This product is for them.

JSON.brief: A simple text format for structured data. Used here for the prompt library, decisions, and workflow scaffolds.

MDX.brief: Markdown with embedded React components. The landing-page copy and blog posts are written in MDX.

OAuth.brief: How you log into a service via another (e.g., 'Sign in with Google'). The setup uses it to connect GitHub.

OG image.brief: Open Graph image. The thumbnail that shows up when someone shares the URL on Twitter, LinkedIn, or in iMessage.

RAG.brief: Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Letting Claude search your own files before answering. Skipped in v1.0 — CLAUDE.md does the same job for most projects.

build id.brief: A short hash that identifies the version of the site your browser is showing. The chip in the utility bar updates when a new deploy lands; the toast offers a refresh.
